blocks.content: Daisy's name was on the buddy list. Not away — online. He stared at it for a long time. Then he opened a window.
The message sat there. Delivered. She was online — he could see it. One minute. Two.
I should have told you myself. I know that.
The cursor blinked in the empty reply box. He watched it. He typed another line and deleted it. Typed it again.

He sat in front of the screen for a while after that. The buddy list was quiet — Eli had gone to bed, Savannah wasn't on. Brooke's screen name showed active but he didn't open it. He looked at the empty window — Daisy's name greyed out at the top — and the three messages he'd sent sitting there unanswered, and the nothing where her words would have been.
He knew she wouldn't see it.
